title: 3Blue1Brown long-form reconstruction set
claim_scope: Public, source-available development set. This is a harness-development benchmark, not an unseen generalization benchmark.
public_original_policy: Embed creator-hosted media only: an official lesson clip when that exact clip is the reconstruction unit, otherwise the creator-hosted YouTube video. Do not rehost the original in the public website.

adapter_smoke.note: The first two provider responses accepted the MP4 but failed the output contract (truncated JSON, then a top-level array); responseSchema made the third attempt valid.
Schema-constrained native-video perception completed on the first attempt.
GPT-5.5 received five ordered temporal JPEG samples. This proves frame-packet perception, not native MP4 input.

feedback_loop: model extracts a time-coded visual signal inventory and decomposes the complete video into one or more scenes
model reads the pinned Manim repository context and its own isolated extension workspace
model emits a multi-scene package, composition timeline, and optional reusable functions inside its own namespace
external network-disabled sandbox renders every scene and composes one candidate video
harness computes deterministic media, layout, text, color, motion, and timing evidence
the same candidate model receives paired reference/candidate evidence and writes a ranked error diagnosis
model revises code while preserving already-matched signals
harness scores every completed round and selects X
signal_hierarchy: persistent visual vocabulary and object identity
text, formula, color, and local styling

workspace_isolation.round_behavior: A round may read and revise only its own candidate workspace. Every round retains an immutable package snapshot.

execution_summary.note: Empty cells are not fabricated results; they remain not run until a validated artifact exists.

cases.title: Linear transformations and matrices
cases.description: Coordinate-grid deformations turn a matrix from a table of numbers into an action on space.
cases.why_in_set: Tests persistent geometry, camera framing, basis-vector identity, and continuous object transforms.

rounds.judge_summary: The candidate preserves the main temporal idea of a rotating/shearing coordinate grid with a persistent diagonal vector whose color shifts from yellow toward purple. However, the reference’s dense bright blue lattice and white axes are replaced by a much darker, sparser green/gray grid, making the geometry and final visual state substantially mismatched.

scene_plan.purpose: Reconstruct the complete six-second pilot clip.

rounds.critic_summary: The candidate animation correctly captures the linear transformation of the grid and the vector, including the color shift from yellow to purple. However, it fails to render the background grid lines correctly, showing only a sparse set of axes and major lines instead of the full coordinate grid present in the reference.
rounds.changes: Increase the density of the background grid lines to match the reference's coordinate system throughout the transformation.

rounds.judge_summary: The candidate preserves the broad idea of a gridded plane with a transforming arrow and a yellow-to-purple color shift, but it misses the reference's dense rectilinear grid, prominent white axes, and affine-looking deformation. Its grid warps nonlinearly and ends in a substantially different layout, so the temporal state transition and final state do not match well.

scene_plan.purpose: Reconstruct the complete six-second pilot clip.

rounds.critic_summary: The candidate video successfully animates the grid transformation and vector color change, but the grid density and the final transformation state do not match the reference. The candidate's grid lines become too sparse, and the final vector position is incorrect.
rounds.changes: Adjust the linear transformation matrix applied to the grid and vector to match the reference, and ensure the grid line density (number of lines) is consistent with the reference.

rounds.judge_summary: The candidate successfully replicates the linear transformation and vector color change. However, the grid lines are significantly fainter than in the reference video.

scene_plan.purpose: Reconstruct the complete six-second pilot clip.

rounds.critic_summary: The candidate captures the basic temporal idea of a vector changing direction/color over a deforming grid, but the grid is far too dim and lacks the bright blue/white major-axis structure of the reference. The vector motion is roughly aligned, yet the deformation, contrast, and color semantics are visibly weaker, making the final state and persistent grid structure hard to read.
rounds.changes: Increase the grid and axis visibility to match the reference: use bright blue grid lines with clear white major axes throughout the animation, while preserving the same deformation timing.

cases.title: Binary, Hanoi and Sierpinski
cases.description: A recursive puzzle becomes a binary counting process and then a geometric pattern.
cases.why_in_set: Tests discrete state transitions, recursion, repeated motifs, and conceptual transitions between representations.

cases.title: But how does bitcoin actually work?
cases.description: A sequence of trust failures incrementally motivates signatures, distributed ledgers, hashing, and proof of work.
cases.why_in_set: Tests long causal storytelling, evolving diagrams, ledger state, and semantic continuity across many scenes.

cases.title: But what is a neural network?
cases.description: Neuron activations and weighted connections build a visual vocabulary for a digit classifier.
cases.why_in_set: Tests dense graph layout, activation encoding, focus changes, and reuse of a stable visual system.

cases.title: What is backpropagation really doing?
cases.description: Local desired changes propagate backward through a network before notation formalizes the intuition.
cases.why_in_set: Tests attention choreography, causal arrows, local-to-global reasoning, and delayed formula introduction.

cases.title: But what is the Fourier Transform?
cases.description: A time-domain signal is wrapped around a circle so frequency becomes visible as geometric concentration.
cases.why_in_set: Tests synchronized plots, parameter sweeps, traced paths, and a formula grounded in a visual procedure.

cases.title: Divergence and curl
cases.description: Particle flow and tiny probes make two local vector-field measurements physically interpretable.
cases.why_in_set: Tests particle systems, vector fields, local probes, and the transition from intuition to operators.

cases.title: Visualizing quaternions
cases.description: Stereographic projection provides a moving 3D window into four-dimensional multiplication.
cases.why_in_set: Tests 3D camera motion, surfaces, projection, depth cues, and state that cannot be judged frame-by-frame alone.

cases.title: Why do colliding blocks compute pi?
cases.description: A physics simulation is re-expressed as billiards in velocity space to explain a surprising digit count.
cases.why_in_set: Tests physically correct timing, collision events, conserved quantities, and a representation-changing proof.

cases.title: But what is a Fourier series?
cases.description: Heat flow motivates a decomposition that moves from sine waves to rotating vectors and drawn paths.
cases.why_in_set: Tests narrative lineage, formula-to-geometry transitions, many synchronized vectors, and cumulative approximation.
